heading: State capacity building grant program
label: 15901 State capacity building grant program
notes: References in TextThe date of enactment of this section, referred to in subsec. (i), is the date of enactment of , which was approved .Pub. L. 118–272Jan. 4, 2025

children.heading: .—Commission state
children.label: 1 .—Commission state
children.text: The term “Commission State” means a State that contains 1 or more eligible counties.

children.heading: .—Eligible county
children.label: 2 .—Eligible county
children.text: The term “eligible county” means a county described in subchapter II of chapter 157.

children.heading: .—Program
children.label: 3 .—Program
children.text: The term “program” means a State capacity building grant program established by a Commission under subsection (b).

children.heading: .—Establishment
children.label: b .—Establishment
children.text: Each Commission shall establish a State capacity building grant program to provide grants to Commission States in the area served by the Commission for the purposes described in subsection (c).
to better support business retention and expansion in eligible counties;

to create programs to encourage job creation and workforce development in eligible counties, including projects and activities, in coordination with other relevant Federal agencies, to strengthen the water sector workforce and facilitate the sharing of best practices;

children.label: A
children.text: to strengthen the capacity in eligible counties to train new professionals in fields for which there is a shortage of workers;

children.label: B
children.text: to increase local capacity in eligible counties for project management, project execution, and financial management; and

children.label: C
children.text: to leverage funding sources for eligible counties;
to prepare economic and infrastructure plans for eligible counties;
to expand access to high-speed broadband in eligible counties;
to provide technical assistance that results in Commission investments in transportation, water, wastewater, and other critical infrastructure;
to promote workforce development in eligible counties to support resilient infrastructure projects;
to develop initiatives to increase the effectiveness of local development districts in eligible counties; and
to implement new or innovative economic development practices that will better position eligible counties to compete in the global economy.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Funds from a grant under a program may be used to support a project, program, or related expense of the Commission State in an eligible county.

children.label: A
children.text: the purchase of furniture, fixtures, or equipment;

children.label: i
children.text: any State member of the Commission (as described in section 15301(b)(1)(B)); or

children.label: ii
children.text: any State alternate member of the Commission (as described in section 15301(b)(2)(B)); or

children.label: C
children.text: the cost of supplanting existing State programs.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: For each fiscal year, before providing a grant under a program, each Commission State shall provide to the Commission an annual work plan that includes the proposed use of the grant.

children.heading: .—Approval
children.label: 2 .—Approval
children.text: No grant under a program shall be provided to a Commission State unless the Commission has approved the annual work plan of the State.

children.label: A
children.text: the amount paid by the Commission State (including any amounts paid on behalf of the Commission State by a nonprofit organization) for administrative expenses for the applicable fiscal year (as determined under section 15304(c)); bears to

children.label: B
children.text: the amount paid by all Commission States served by the Commission (including any amounts paid on behalf of a Commission State by a nonprofit organization) for administrative expenses for that fiscal year (as determined under that section).

children.heading: .—Requirement
children.label: 2 .—Requirement
children.text: To be eligible to receive a grant under a program for a fiscal year, a Commission State (or a nonprofit organization on behalf of the Commission State) shall pay the amount of administrative expenses of the Commission State for the applicable fiscal year (as determined under section 15304(c)).

children.heading: .—Approval
children.label: 3 .—Approval
children.text: For each fiscal year, a grant provided under a program shall be approved and made available as part of the approval of the annual budget of the Commission.

children.heading: .—Grant Availability
children.label: g .—Grant Availability
children.text: Funds from a grant under a program shall be available only during the fiscal year for which the grant is provided.

children.heading: .—Report
children.label: h .—Report
children.text: Each fiscal year, each Commission State shall submit to the relevant Commission and make publicly available a report that describes the use of the grant funds and the impact of the program in the Commission State.

children.heading: .—Continuation of Program Authority for Northern Border Regional Commission
children.label: i .—Continuation of Program Authority for Northern Border Regional Commission
children.text: With respect to the Northern Border Regional Commission, the program shall be a continuation of the program under section 6304(c) of the Agriculture Improvement Act of 2018 ( note; ) (as in effect on the day before the date of enactment of this section).40 U.S.C. 15501Public Law 115–334
